page_content: ENMA401 Continuum Modeling of Materials (3 Credits) Introduces continuum modeling techniques in materials science and engineering. This course covers and emphasizes the applications of continuum modeling techniques using COMSOL software package in simulating a range of materials phenomena and properties. Speciﬁc topics of continuum modeling include: The construction and analyses of continuum models using COMSOL software package; Structural mechanics; Heat transfer; Electrical current; Chemical species transport; Fluid flow; Multi-physics models coupling above phenomena. Prerequisite: ENMA362, PHYS270, PHYS271, and MATH246; or equivalent; and ENMA165 or MATH206. Restriction: Permission of ENGR-Materials Science & Engineering department. Credit Only Granted for: ENMA401 or ENMA489C. Formerly: ENMA489C.
